item.title: SDFT: Self-Distillation Enables Continual Learning
item.author: xxxx
item.description: How can large models continually learn new knowledge without forgetting old knowledge? The SDFT method proposed by MIT and ETH teams enables on-policy continual learning without reward signals through self-distillation.

item.title: Video-KTR: Token Attribution-Driven Video Reasoning Enhancement
item.author: xxxx
item.description: Video-KTR proposes a modality-aware token-level reinforcement learning framework that identifies three types of key tokens—visual, temporal, and entropy—through counterfactual perturbation, performing policy updates only on key tokens. Surpassing GPT-4o with 42.7% on Video-Holmes benchmark, it provides new insights for fine-grained RL in video reasoning.

item.title: Self-Distillation Trilogy: From Continual Learning to Inference Optimization
item.author: xxxx
item.description: Three papers on Self-Distillation explore the core idea of letting models teach themselves from perspectives of continual learning, mathematical reasoning, and code generation

item.title: Concept-Aware Fine-tuning
item.author: xxxx
item.description: In vertical domains, how can fine-tuning help improve conceptual understanding?

item.title: LoRA without Regret
item.author: xxxx
item.description: Thinking Machines’ latest blog finally demystifies LoRA

item.title: RL's Razor
item.author: xxxx
item.description: Verified through solid evidence that RL forgets less than SFT because on-policy training inherently has an implicit low KL constraint, making the KL divergence between the model’s output distribution after RL and before RL smaller.

item.title: Qwen3Guard's Key Technical Details
item.author: xxxx
item.description: In this post we summarize the key technical insights of Qwen3Guard …
